TextENGNew: ――It is a tale from long, long ago. A fairytale.
A wondrous story born from the slightest 'difference.'
changeReason: Old says 'A mysterious tale'; new says 'A wondrous story.' JPN 不思議な物語 means 'wondrous/mysterious story,' but 'mysterious' implies unknown/puzzling while 'wondrous' implies marvelous. Both are valid but convey different tones

TextENGNew: A story of the bonds of a single family—who lived with all their hearts for others, and prayed with all their hearts for others……
changeReason: Old says 'a family'; new says 'a single family.' JPN ひとつの家族 explicitly says 'one/single family,' emphasizing its singularity

TextENGNew: …It was, truly, a scene of utter carnage.
changeReason: Old says 'a truly gruesome sight'; new says 'a scene of utter carnage.' JPN 凄惨な光景 means 'a gruesome/devastating scene' -- 'carnage' implies mass violence specifically, a stronger and more specific word than 'gruesome sight'

TextENGNew: The trees and grasses burned.
The earth was scorched to cinder.
Ash and debris danced upon the searing fumes, while crimson flames devoured trees, houses, and fields alike, illuminating the dark night as though it were high noon.
And covering the ground, piled atop one another in dizzying numbers, were the remains of living creatures reduced to masses of flesh and blood.
Immeasurable terror and despair had taken root there, and the stench of death—scorched and fetid—blanketed the eye's every reach.
Grrrr… Grrrrrr…!!
Countless towering behemoths gathered around the blazing flames, growling in chorus.
From their arms—thick and corded as tree trunks—long, razor-sharp claws extended.
Their mouths, split back to their ears, bared fangs of wicked, sharpened malice.
Attended by countless followers and servants, they were a clan that had lorded absolute power and terror over village after village for generations… the so-called clan of 'demons.'

On mere whim they would descend upon human settlements, lay waste to crops, and slaughter both man and beast for their hunger—to the powerless people of this land, they were beings to be dreaded above all.
…And yet so many of those 'demons' now lay strewn about—limbs severed, hearts pierced—reduced to mute heaps of flesh.
A sight befitting the phrase, in its most literal sense: …a field of corpses.
If the word 'nightmare' could take form and walk this world… it would be the spectacle unfolding before one's very eyes, at this very moment.

Having always believed themselves to possess matchless, absolute power, they could not accept this reality—and so they did not merely falter, but trembled in fear, unable even to move from where they stood.
Ghkk… Ghghhhh…!!
A short distance behind those 'demons,' there loomed yet another 'demon'—a full measure larger than the rest.
It understood human language, possessed the reason to restrain its murderous instincts, and yet held power that far surpassed any of the others—this was the one the villagers called the 'chief.'
The other 'demons' too obeyed it as their leader, never doubting its absolute strength and menace.
Ghhh, grrrrrrrrr…!!
But now, within the 'chief's' low, heavy growl, uncertainty could be heard… and something beyond it—fear—woven through the sound.
It had already reached the level of trembling, and only grew stronger from the moment it sensed a 'presence' moving within the flames that still roared and burned.
(…As expected of the strongest Observer of the 'Putus.' It seems that was not enough to fell her…!)
Yet as if to shake off that unease, the 'demons' fixed their malevolent eyes all at once upon the blazing wall of fire, growling as they bared their claws and fangs, waiting.
Every muscle in their bodies was coiled to the point of splitting—saturated with brute, savage power.
Every joint groaned as the urge for slaughter and feral violence swelled and swelled inside them.
Yet their bodies shuddered with fine tremors, and the pounding of their hearts only grew louder and louder…
The sweat that dripped ceaselessly from head to foot was no longer produced only by the heat of the surrounding flames and the fury of battle.
At last, a figure rose from within the flames… and 'it' revealed itself.
With unhurried steps, cloak trailing behind it, long hair—deepest violet-black—danced in the wind.
And from its head grew two horns, just like those of the 'demons,' sharp and ominous, as if to flaunt their menace.
Treading the scorched earth with slow, deliberate steps, 'it' advanced toward the 'demons.'
…At first glance, a slender figure—about the height and build of a human woman.
Yet from the end of that slender, elegantly extended arm, straight out like a willow bough, gleamed a single sword—long as a willow leaf, radiant and keen.
And from its tip, even now… an unceasing torrent of warm blood dripped down.
At that moment, one of the building's few remaining pillars crumbled, and the flame surged a brief, fierce instant in the draft.
As the renewed blaze lit the figure's back and illuminated that face—expressionless as a Noh mask—… the 'demons' shuddered.
Ghhkk?! Ghghhhh…!!
…Unbelievable. No—it should not have been believable.
Completely… unharmed.
Breathing unperturbed, not a shade of agitation on that face—and the only trace of anything amiss was the thin coat of dust and spattered blood on the cloak.
And those eyes—until now veiled in shadow—gleamed scarlet… sharp, and cold.
Holding a radiance of cold cruelty, they looked out over the assembled 'demons' with a haughty stare that made even them flinch.
(Even our death-trap, set to be certain… utterly useless!)
…The preparations for ambush had been thorough, and things had proceeded exactly as planned.
They had lured 'it' to the village official's estate they had commandeered as their stronghold, then encircled it with overwhelming numbers.
And when the moment arrived, they had triggered the hidden explosives—blowing the entire grounds to splinters——
As the price of that gambit, roughly half their number—those too slow to escape, or those given no time at all—had been swallowed by the flash and shockwave and vanished without a trace.
The 'it' they sought to destroy should have been blocked by the 'demons,' left dead or at least fatally wounded… or so it should have been.
But——
"——Is this… what you called your… trump card?"
The face illuminated by the firelight bore no expression of pain—indeed, no expression at all.
No anger. No pity. …Not even a trace of a smile.
And those eyes—jade-bright—had been stained the color of blood.
…Sharp as a blade, burning with fierce light, they fixed a cold stare upon the 'demons.'
(……You monster…!!)
"…I may not look it, but I am the inheritor of the Jedha name—one of the eighteen Ryuun Hai. I am not so diminished as to be felled by the clever schemes of fallen wretches such as yourselves."
Ghhh, grrrrrrrr…!!
"That said… to conspire against even the lives of your own followers, to use them as bait— …that is a whim rather too excessive."
Ghkk, gheeee…, gheeee!!
Unable to endure the crushing weight of fear and despair, the maddened 'demons' charged with a roar toward 'it'——toward Jedha.
Their movements were swift as a gale, closing the distance at a speed the eye could not track.
The moment they drew near, they swung their massive claws, bared their razor fangs, and launched a single, overwhelming assault——!
"A simultaneous charge— …not bad."
Using numbers and momentum to catch the opponent off guard—then unleashing a ferocious onslaught with sheer ferocity, allowing no opening for a battle cry or counterattack.
The bewilderment born of that would cloud judgment.
Hesitation grown from indecision scatters attention.
"But——"
Gyaaaaaagh!!
Grghyaaaooogh!!
"…To fail to gauge the difference in power between us makes you nothing but brutes."
…Jedha's sword strokes had been 'imperceptible' to the eye.
Only the afterimage of a flash of light seared itself onto their retinas—but even the 'chief's' eyes could not perceive how Jedha had swung the blade, nor how she had cut them down.
Furthermore—
What reached the senses was the delayed sound of each slash, and the death cries that followed.
And… at the same moment those sounds arrived, the earth shook again, and again, in relentless succession.
The 'demons' had been struck down—one, then another…!
Ggheeegh!!
What was clearly visible was that figure, approaching one step, then another, unhurried and certain.
With each footstep, sprays of blood scattered and dissolved into a dark-red mist.
The raw, iron stench—like old blood—slowly, inexorably grew stronger.
Gggyaaooogh!!
From time to time that figure swayed, side to side, like a mirage…
Yet only those eyes remained unwavering—trained straight on the 'chief,' blazing with fierce, piercing light.
Like a dream… yes, like watching a nightmare.
Faced with this, the 'chief' found his limbs would not obey him—his breath stopped, and even his thoughts ground to a halt.
Before long, when the sound of breathing unlike his own was close enough for the 'chief' himself to hear——
Jedha suddenly stopped.
"……Hmph."
With that quiet breath, Jedha shook the blood from her blade… and turned its tip, sharp and gleaming, toward the chief.
Snapping back to his senses and looking about him… he found every 'demon' that had surrounded them was now felled by Jedha——
That he and Jedha were the last ones standing upon this earth… the 'chief' only now, finally, understood.
(So the rumors were true after all… ——But!!!)
A moment—
In the instant Jedha drew breath and her stance hardened, the 'chief' kicked off the earth and closed the gap at once.
And before she could set a guard to meet him, he raised his massive arm, flashed his long sharp claws, and slashed at the figure standing defenseless before him in close range.
——But.
"——Too slow."
Jedha was no longer there—his palm drove through empty air and slammed into the bare ground.
And then.
"——Pray, villain…!!"
The instant he realized that whisper had come from behind him, the 'chief'—gripped by a bone-deep chill—threw himself sideways before he even thought to turn around.
In that same instant, a single streak of light blazed—then, a beat later, the sound of air being cleaved shook the eardrums.
And a moment after that… the great tree that had stood near where the 'chief' had just been planted itself fell sideways with a dull, heavy crash.
"As expected of the 'Putus'—it has been a very long time since someone dodged me."
"…The first strike, that is."
Murmuring that… Jedha swept back her hair, and at the same instant—
(Ghh…?!  Gghaaaaaagh!!)
The moment the 'chief' felt a searing, razor pain at his right shoulder and the base of his left leg… bright blood erupted.
…He had only dodged the first blow.
Jedha had used the opening as the 'chief' lost his footing to slash at him twice more…!
And what was more—what was more!
Those wounds had severed arteries as though aimed precisely, forcing a torrent of blood despite the cuts not being so deep…!
(I can't… read her swordsmanship?  Impossible!  We're both 'Putus'—how can there be this much difference between us…?!)
Forgetting the pouring blood and the agonizing pain, the 'chief' trembled in stunned disbelief.
The 'chief' carried the same Ryuun blood as Jedha, however different their standing… and yet this gulf between them existed, immovable and plain.

Even if she were the strongest, exhaustion wears on any body and wounds accumulate. So by wearing her down with a stratagem, then swarming her immediately with superior numbers, she could surely be defeated.
Only now did the 'chief' finally understand how naive that thinking had been…!
Gaaah, …gaaaaaagh!!
The 'chief,' driven past desperation into sheer panic, flailed his arms and charged.
Each sweeping flash of his claws became a gale that split empty air.
Each crashing blow gouged the hardened earth and left deep claw marks.
But… they did not connect. They hit nothing.
changeReason: Old says 'he missed. He hit no one' (singular male subject); new says 'they did not connect. They hit nothing' (plural, referring to attacks). JPN uses 当たらない (they don't hit) referring to his attacks, new is more accurate

TextENGNew: His attack, thrown with every last shred of his strength—
…His arms, his claws, his fangs—those very weapons that had brought unspeakable death and terror to this village and the settlements beyond—
…Were utterly powerless.
And what was more, his attacks were not even being dodged or parried——!
(Why… why won't these land?!!)
changeReason: Old says 'Why can't I hit her?'; new says 'why won't these land?!' Old uses 'hit her' (targeting the person), new uses 'these land' (referring to attacks). JPN 当たらない is about attacks connecting, new is more accurate

TextENGNew: The 'chief' raged and again and again, unleashing roars that seemed to tear his throat raw while slashing and slashing at Jedha over and over… and yet she passed through every blade like a phantom.
Not her legs.
Not her arms.
Not even her face.
Not even the long hair dancing in the darkness, not even the cloak swaying with her movement, could so much as be grazed.
And in every small opening he created, Jedha's strokes came without fail—piercing his limbs, crushing his joints, severing nerves—gradually stealing his ability to move…!
"Too slow."
"Over here."
"Wide open."
Gyaaaaagghhh!!
…It was not a matter of speed.
It was something else—a difference in the very 'dimension' of an ability they did not even possess.
Gaaaaaaaaghhh!!
And then, with the tendons of both legs severed, the 'chief' could stand no longer—he sank to his knees and crumpled to the ground.
He could no longer raise his arms… to say nothing of swinging them.

…Yet Jedha merely looked down at him where he lay, battered and broken, from point-blank range with unhurried calm, the now-crimson blade extended toward his face, and declared, with cold arrogance: '…Stand.'
"…What's wrong? Is that the end of you?"
changeReason: Old says 'That all you got?'; new says 'Is that the end of you?' JPN お前の全力 means 'your full power/all you have' vs implying finality of 'end of you'

TextENGNew: Ghhh, ghooogh…!

"Is that all you have? Is that truly all you are?  …No—I think not. One who carries Ryuun blood, who dares to call himself 'Putus,' who sacrificed so many lives to arrive here—it cannot be that this is all you amount to."
(Ghhh, ghhhh…!!)
"Come now. Show me your full power, here and now.  …Stand. I left your claws and fangs 'unbroken' precisely for this——"
(S-she… she still has reserve left?!)
"Ryuun law demands equal measure in sin and punishment.  …Therefore, you have a duty."

"Just as with the wretched humans you have hunted, just as with the kinsmen you led to ruin— it is my duty to take my fill of pleasure from you, to let you serve as brief consolation in the long turning of the years."
"That is the fitting retribution for what you are…!"
Grghyaaoogh!!

"…Now. Rise.  Then dance for me, perform for me.  Burn your soul to cinders, wring the blood from your veins, and fight.  Fight as your blood and instinct bid you to.  Fight with hatred. Fight with resentment.  Fight. Fight."
"…And die by my hand!!"
(R-retreat!!  Alone I cannot prevail… the gulf is too great!!)
Hiding his fear no longer, the 'chief' kicked off the ground, wheeled about, and bolted in the direction opposite Jedha.
But——
(Gwaaaaagghhh!!)
"…You have no right to flee."
Jedha closed the distance in the blink of an eye, and her thrust from behind drove into the 'chief's' back and through his lung.
In that instant, blood fountained from his torn mouth, and the world before him turned red.
"…Jedha does not attempt. Jedha finishes.  Pass judgment, or be judged.  Kill, or be killed.  …That is the lot of an 'Observer'—and the weight of my own transgression."
Ghkk, …ghee…!!
"You, who attacked and preyed upon the innocent and powerless of this land—toying with them and slaughtering them.  You, who led kinsmen living in peace astray and drove them to violence."

"…That sin is grave, and all but unforgivable.  Therefore, dig up every error of your past from the depths of memory, and spend what time remains suffering and writhing as you repent of your crimes alongside your own existence…"
With those words, Jedha wrenched her blade free, went around to face the 'chief' as he gasped and doubled over in agony and suffocation… and began to murmur a chant, barely above breath.
As though responding to it, the armored sleeve that had covered her left forearm dissolved away, baring the white skin beneath.
Then, after roughly wiping the blood from the blade with her cloak, Jedha drew her right foot back into a half-stance, extended her left arm forward, and traced the sword quietly along it.
Blood welled from the thin cut she had drawn, and stained the blade again in mottled red.
Then Jedha drew her right hand back as though pulling a longbow, and set herself in a straight, one-handed thrusting stance.
changeReason: Old says 'holding her sword aloft in a thrusting stance'; new says 'a straight, one-handed thrusting stance.' JPN 片手突き means 'one-handed thrust,' new is more precise

TextENGNew: "…I, inheritor of the Jedha name, one of the eighteen Ryuun Hai, ask this of you now in accordance with Ryuun Clossus Regulation Thirty-Five."
changeReason: Old references 'regulation 35'; new references 'Regulation 5.' JPN says 第３５詞 (article/verse 35), which old had correct but new changed to 5. The number in the source is 35

TextENGNew: "The treasures granted by heaven to we of the Ryuun people are a proud past, a beautiful present, and a fulfilled future…"
Gaaagh, gaaaaaagh…!
"Yet one who has broken the law is permitted only one of these three.  …I ask you once, and once only.  Which do you desire—the past, the present… or the future?"
changeReason: Old says 'three treasures from the heavens'; new removes the number 'three.' JPN lists three items without explicitly saying 'three'; old added a number not in the source while new omits it

TextENGNew: Ghee… ghghee…!!
"'Spare me'… is it."
Ghkk, …ghkk…!!
"'Forgive me'… is it."
With each whispered word, the light in Jedha's eyes grew sharper and more intense.
There was no anger in it.
No joy.
Only the colorless, transparent pride of an 'Observer'—and an executioner.
And so——
"…What did you do, for all those who pleaded the very same of you?"
There was no room in her for any mitigating consideration, nor any ear to turn toward a condemned man's pleas for mercy.
"Stars for the sky, flowers for the green.  For wicked dreams—a poem of silence.  Your future already lies within the void.  ——Therefore, fall here——!!"
Gaaaaaaghhh, gaaaaaaaaaaaghhh!!
"Hai-Ryuun Yeasomul Jedha.  That is the name of the one who comes for you."
"——You fool who has desecrated the name of our kin. …Pass on, soul and all."
"——Aaaaahhh!!"
Gyaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaghhh!!!
"…Revival is impossible, then."
Peering at the face of a man lying on the floor in the shadow of a collapsing wall… Jedha exhaled.
His wounds were deeper than any of the other corpses, the blood loss severe—and the medulla oblongata had been destroyed.
He had likely never learned to fight, yet judging from the state of his battered body, the desperate will to fulfill his duty to protect what was dear to him came through clearly…
'…Yeasomul Jedha, prepare to withdraw.  The remaining explosives set inside the building will soon ignite.'
"I know.  …What became of the evacuees?"
'No problems.  Shezhen Jedha has eliminated all pursuit.  No trace of any Grifys remains anywhere.'
"…I see."
Having sent her reply to the 'voice' that reached her directly in her mind, Jedha nonetheless did not turn back—and headed deeper into the building.
During the fighting just now, for the briefest instant… she had sensed something inside this house.
(If my instincts are right, there should be survivors somewhere in this vicinity…)
And beyond that, the presence of the man's corpse just outside—evidence of resistance fought to the last—lent further weight to the possibility.
So though Jedha had been on the verge of departing, she changed her mind—deciding to search a little further—and stepped deeper into the room.
Suddenly, something on the floor caught her eye—she crouched down and picked it up.
…It was a small doll.
And not far from where it had lain, a single floorboard was slightly raised.
Finding it out of place, Jedha pried it open… and beneath the floorboard was a small storage space.
Peering inside, she could see the shape of a woman crumpled in the corner.
"Ah…"
And cradled in that woman's arms, protected, was an infant.
The woman was likely the child's mother… her bleeding was severe, and she had already stopped breathing. …Yet the child, somehow, appeared to still be alive.
"Mmm…"
Jedha was about to reach in at once to help—but then she stopped her outstretched arm, and hesitated.

Life for the people who lived in this land was by no means easy. She would not go so far as to call them poor, but providing food for themselves and their families was already more than enough to manage.
And with the chaos of these events only just ending—it would likely take years just to return to the level they had known.
In such circumstances, what lay ahead for a nursing infant who had lost both parents and had no means of sustenance could easily be imagined.
Almost certainly, harder trials than death itself would be waiting……
(In that case—better to let it go with its mother——)
That was what she whispered within herself.
If nothing else, she would grant it a clean end—Jedha raised the sword she held.
Then set the tip straight toward the child's throat——
'……Jedha?'
"Ryuun-Ohc.  Do you know where the survivors in this area took shelter?"
'…What are you intending to do?'
"A whim. It will need some embellishing, I suppose."
Saying so, Jedha gathered the infant from its mother's chest. Then, with gentle fingers, she wiped the blood and grime from that small face.
Now it is a story of long ago.
changeReason: Old says 'This story has long since come and gone'; new says 'Now it is a story of long ago.' JPN とても古い昔 means 'very long ago,' framing differs from 'come and gone'

TextENGNew: One day, wicked demons came to this land, crossing distant mountains.
changeReason: Old says 'Evil demons suddenly crossed distant mountains'; new says 'One day, wicked demons came.' JPN 突然 means suddenly, but new adds 'One day' framing. Minor temporal framing change

TextENGNew: The demons stole food, preyed upon livestock, and ran rampant, doing terrible things to the villagers.
But the villagers could do nothing. For those who tried to protect them were killed and had their living innards devoured.
And so the villagers began to live hidden from the demons, always shut away inside their homes.
Not only at night, but in the day as well.
On rainy days, and on fair ones alike.
…Even so, the demons attacked their homes as though taking sport in the hunt, and went on killing villagers without end.
At last, the crops in the fields were gone, and the stores inside every home were nearly spent…
Then one day—just as they had resigned themselves to death——
From the swamp on the edge of the village, a god appeared.
The god bore horns, just as the demons did. And so at first the villagers trembled, thinking another demon had come.
But the god did not attack the people.
Instead, it wielded a divine blade, slaying the demons one after another until at last it struck down even their king.
Afterward, the god came before one of the villagers who had taken refuge in a cave in the mountains, and holding out an infant, it spoke…

"My soul dwells within this child.  Therefore, raise this child well, and reverence it always—do not let that bloodline be extinguished.  Do this, and I will protect you from the demons, and this village shall prosper for eternity……"
The infant was a boy.
The villagers gave their heartfelt thanks to the god who had saved their village, and heeding its words, resolved to raise the child with care as the reincarnation of their guardian deity.
And so they named the swamp from which the horned god had appeared 'Onigafuchi,' and in time the village that the god had protected came to be called 'Onigafuchi Village'…
——The Legend of Onigafuchi Village: 'Oyashiro-sama.'
